factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings & AI talent exodus
factors.summary: Alphabet lost $225B market cap after two AI researchers, including a Nobel laureate, left for rival labs. Microsoft, Amazon, Netflix all fell sharply (-3.18%, -4.75%, -5.82% respectively). Salesforce posted 14th consecutive day of losses on AI momentum concerns.

factors.name: Fed hawkish repricing & real yields rising
factors.summary: Chicago Fed's Goolsbee stated inflation is well above 2% target and heading wrong direction, particularly services. US Treasury yields firm near 101.00 USD Index, pressuring long-duration growth multiples in Nasdaq names.

factors.name: Single-name momentum kills (SpaceX, Oracle, Tesla)
factors.summary: SpaceX tanked 16.43% post-IPO rally collapse; Oracle cut 21,000 employees; Tesla faces federal safety probe after fatal Model 3 crash in Texas. Negative sentiment on mega-cap execution and governance.

factors.name: Liquidity squeeze warnings (Morgan Stanley)
factors.summary: Reserve Management Program down 75% from peak, Treasury buybacks halved, accelerating lending growth creating pincer effect on capital. Balance-sheet tailwind that supported equities fading fast.

factors.name: USD strength near 13-month highs
factors.summary: DXY near 101.00 after Iran deal optimism shifted risk repricing. Stronger dollar headwind for Nasdaq's 40%+ international revenue exposure, particularly software/cloud.

factors.name: Iran peace progress = risk-off for duration assets
factors.summary: US-Iran talks flagged progress, Strait of Hormuz opening normalized. Oil fell ~3%, reducing inflation hedge narrative that supported tech multiple expansion. Geopolitical premium unwinding.

factors.name: Nasdaq volume & breadth deterioration
factors.summary: Index fell -1.32% with concentrated losses in GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, NVDA cohort. Multiple mega-caps down 4-6% on same day signals broad sentiment shift, not isolated story.

factors.name: IPO pipeline risk (SpaceX, OpenAI coming)
factors.summary: MarketWatch notes 40% crash risk lurking in IPO pipeline, comparing cycle to 1929 and 2000. SpaceX already showing post-IPO weakness despite $100.8B cash; OpenAI/Modular ($4B Qualcomm deal) could trigger cap rotation.

factors.name: Alphabet AI researcher departures
factors.summary: Two high-profile AI exits from Google DeepMind signal talent drain and competitive loss to OpenAI/Anthropic. Market pricing in structural AI race concern for once-dominant player.

factors.name: No offsetting bullish catalysts in news flow
factors.summary: No mega earnings surprises, no Fed dovish pivot, no surprising PCE beat. Qualcomm-Modular M&A (+$4B capex) small relative to downsides. Crypto tax bills, quantum orders minor.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 has rolled over sharply following Alphabet's $225B wipeout on AI talent exodus and broad mega-cap execution concerns. Fed hawkish repricing (real yields rising, Goolsbee's inflation commentary) combined with liquidity squeeze warnings from Morgan Stanley and 75% drawdown in Fed balance-sheet support is compressing growth multiples. Iran peace deal optimism is reducing geopolitical premium and unwinding the inflation-hedge narrative that supported tech valuations; USD strength near 13-month highs adds headwind to international revenue. SpaceX post-IPO collapse, Tesla federal probe, Oracle layoffs, and Salesforce's 14-day loss streak signal deteriorating single-name momentum. Market is in PHASE 2 pullback, having already moved -1.32% intraday with no rebound structure yet, suggesting further consolidation or breakdown likely before buyers step in.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation data (next ~24-48h) — hot reading would confirm Fed hawkish repricing, bearish for NQ
Flash PMI Asia/Eurozone (Tuesday 23 June) — weak data could indicate slower growth, bearish equities
Fed speaker calendar (Warsh Humphrey-Hawkins testimony) — any hawkish rate-path signals would pressure multiples
Qualcomm-Modular deal close / FTC antitrust review timing
Nvidia earnings expectations and hyperscaler cloud capex guidance (forward calendar item, likely July)
SpaceX bond settlement completion and secondary share action
Treasury yield moves (10Y critical threshold around 4.25-4.35%)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would flip BULLISH if: (1) PCE comes in significantly cooler than expected (sub-2.5% core), triggering Fed dovish repricing and real-yield compression; (2) Major mega-cap earnings beat (MSFT, AAPL, NVDA) in coming weeks with strong cloud/AI capex guidance; (3) Iran deal collapses and oil re-spikes, reinflating inflation hedge premium on tech; (4) Nasdaq finds support and stabilizes above 30,200 with volume, indicating buyers stepping in; (5) VIX drops below 14 and options gamma becomes less negative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early-phase (down 1.32% intraday, -0.33% 24h), consolidating after Alphabet shock. Wait for pullback retest to 30,500-30,600 resistance before considering short entry, or break below 30,100 support with volume for continuation short. Do not chase lower in thin overnight hours; prefer US cash open confirmation.

factors.name: Tech Sector Profit-Taking
factors.summary: South Korea's KOSPI slumped 6%+ with explicit chip selloff (SK Hynix ETF assets surging signals leverage unwind risk). FXStreet reports 'AI rally cools' across Asia as investors rotate out of this year's best-performing tech shares.

factors.name: Hawkish Fed Repricing
factors.summary: Fed rate hike bets remain elevated per FXStreet; DXY holding near 13-month highs (~101.00). Non-yielding assets (gold, silver, crypto) under pressure as real yields remain attractive; long-duration growth multiples vulnerable.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD/JPY near 161.55 with intervention fears; USD/IDR at 17,900; DXY holding firm. Strong dollar headwind for NDX multinationals' international revenue translation (c.40-50% of megacap earnings typically from overseas).

factors.name: China Consumption Malaise
factors.summary: China's 618 shopping festival growth 'slows sharply' per Bloomberg; consumption 'remains subdued.' Direct hit to Apple, Amazon, and cloud/semiconductor demand from Chinese tech sector.

factors.name: Valuation Overstretch & Leverage Unwind
factors.summary: SK Hynix leveraged ETF ($17B, largest in Hong Kong) and margin liquidation signals suggest AI capex rally may have run too far too fast. MarketWatch warns of 40% crash risk in IPO pipeline (SpaceX, OpenAI float risk).

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Optimism (Short-Term Risk-Off)
factors.summary: VP Vance signals 'very good progress' in talks; US lifted 60-day Iran oil sanctions waiver. De-risking of Middle East conflict removes safe-haven bid; aluminum fell to 3-month low; oil consolidating near $74. Risk-off unwind likely to favor bonds over growth.

factors.name: Inflation Input Costs Rising
factors.summary: Japan PMI input costs at 4-year peak (5 consecutive months of acceleration per investinglive); Middle East energy/raw material cost surge. Real yields remain elevated; stagflation risk > disinflation.

factors.name: Prior SELL6/10 Bias (2026-06-23 00:05)
factors.summary: Previous read was SELL 6/10 with no confirmation triggered. Move has continued modestly lower (-0.33% in 24h), suggesting bias remains valid but lacks explosive follow-through.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window Closed (Near-Term)
factors.summary: No imminent mega-cap earnings announcements in next 24-48h per visible calendar. Cloud capex guidance window remains open but lacks fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Liquidity Squeeze Warning (Morgan Stanley)
factors.summary: Reserve Management Program down ~75% from peak; Treasury buybacks cut 50%; accelerating lending growth absorbs capital. Structural balance-sheet tailwind fading—negative for long-duration growth stocks.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is experiencing a tactical pullback from its AI-driven rally peak as profit-taking accelerates, particularly in semiconductor and cloud-cap stocks (KOSPI -6%, SK Hynix leverage unwind visible). The confluence of hawkish Fed repricing, resilient dollar strength (~101 DXY), deteriorating China consumption, and rising input inflation is creating a mid-term headwind for long-duration growth valuations. While US-Iran peace optimism provides near-term relief (de-risks geopolitical premium), it simultaneously removes the safe-haven bid that had supported equities, favoring bonds instead. The liquidity squeeze (fading Reserve Management Program, accelerating lending growth) is a structural constraint on equity enthusiasm.
catalysts_ahead: Australia May CPI (morning Asia session Tuesday) — if trimmed mean >3.6% YoY, signals stickier inflation; RBA on hold/higher bias risks broader EM carry unwind
UK political uncertainty (Starmer resignation risk) — spillover volatility to tech sector if GBP/USD weakens further below 1.3200 (cross-asset dislocation risk)
Fed speakers mid-week — any reaffirmation of 'higher for longer' narrative will re-anchor real yields, extend equity selloff
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL earnings window (typically late June) — forward cloud capex guidance is make-or-break for tech multiple re-rating
Oil/Energy continuation (Brent $80 floor per Danske Bank) — sustained energy inflation will pressure NQ margin outlooks
VIX/VXN expansion tracking — if vol breaks 16-17 to the upside, forced selling in call spreads could accelerate NAS100 correction
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL) deliver upside guidance and hyperscaler cloud capex forecasts are raised sharply, and if Fed speakers signal dovish pivot (lower real yields), the bear case collapses and NAS100 rallies back through 30,700-31,000. Additionally, if US-Iran peace deal is signed and Middle East risk premium collapses entirely (oil crashes <$70), risk-on sentiment could return rapidly, favoring growth. Finally, if China stimulus (fiscal or monetary) is announced in next 48-72h, consumption malaise narrative may reverse.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already 0.33% lower in 24h with soft 4h momentum (+0.20%). This is PHASE_2 (pullback underway but not extended yet). Entry: SHORT on rallies toward 30,500-30,700 resistance with stop above 30,800; or WAIT for further breakdown below 30,100 support to confirm continuation and avoid late-chase risk. Avoid chasing shorts at current levels given lack of follow-through volume. Intraday traders should monitor VXN for break above 17.0 (technical capitulation) before adding shorts.

factors.name: Tech Sector Rotation & Selloff
factors.summary: SpaceX dropped >3% post-IPO after $400B selloff; US consumer and communication services names under pressure per Danske Bank. Tech-led rotation underway with US futures down 2% on 'tech worries' per news.

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Earnings Risk
factors.summary: No major NDX component earnings in today's news, but sentiment sour on megacap tech broadly. Risk of guidance cuts on capex uncertainty and AI demand normalization.

factors.name: Real Yields & Fed Hike Bets
factors.summary: News reports 'Fed hike bets' driving concern. Futures down 2% on this concern; rising real yields compress long-duration growth valuations that dominate NAS100.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY hovering near 13-month high (~101.13); strong USD headwind for NDX component international revenues (~40-50% of mega-cap earnings).

factors.name: Eurozone PMI Weakness & Global Recession Risk
factors.summary: UK services PMI 48.7 vs 50.1 expected (contraction); Germany composite 48.0 vs 49.6 expected; Eurozone contraction signals ripple to US tech demand. Economic slowdown backdrop pressures growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran Peace Talks)
factors.summary: US-Iran waiver unlocks Iranian oil sales; oil price collapse (WTI $72.75, down 3% multi-day) eases inflation fears slightly but removes energy risk premium. Mixed for tech: lower inflation is dovish-equity, but flight-to-safety rally absent.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell below $63,000 amid tech selloff; 'altcoin season' signal flashed but driven by BTC slide. Crypto-equity correlation positive in downturns; tech-BTC linkage reinforces NDX weakness.

factors.name: VIX / Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Defensive positioning noted; dips 'not being bought today' per Bloomberg. Apollo restricting private credit redemptions (17% exit requests) signals liquidity stress in credit markets—tail-risk for equities.

factors.name: No Major Bullish Catalyst in News
factors.summary: AI capex commentary absent; no hyperscaler forward guidance or semicon upside surprises. Hyperscaler earnings not imminent in this window. Vacuum of bullish news.

key_levels.support: 30,100 (today's low likely near here); 30,000 round psychological

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in the early stages of a tech sector rotation and valuation reset, triggered by Fed rate hike concerns and megacap earnings uncertainty. Eurozone PMI misses underscore global growth headwinds, while a strong US Dollar and weak crypto sentiment amplify risk-off moves into growth equities. Geopolitical relief (Iran deal) has failed to spark a sustainable rally; instead, the absence of new bullish catalysts in AI/capex guidance leaves the index vulnerable to further consolidation and potential retests of support. The prior two Haiku reads (SELL 6/10, SELL 5/10) align with today's weakness, but price action has only declined 0.33% in 24h—not yet an extended downmove.
catalysts_ahead: US Flash PMI (Services & Composite) – due later today; Eurozone PMI released; weaker read could trigger fresh selling
Fed speakers / policy speculation – continued focus on rate trajectory
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings guidance (exact dates not in news but likely within 2-4 weeks)
Treasury yields & 10Y – real yield moves dominate NDX sensitivity
China economic data (618 shopping festival weakness noted; more data incoming)
Any major antitrust or regulatory news on mega-caps (DOJ, EU DMA)
screening.risk_warning: Risk invalidating SELL bias: (1) Fed signals dovish pivot / rate cut expectations reverse (would fuel growth multiple re-expansion), (2) Surprise strong US PMI or inflation print cooler than expected (could stabilize yields and attract tech buyers), (3) Mega-cap earnings beat with bullish AI/capex guidance (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL guidance would reignite momentum), (4) Volatility spike triggers mechanical flows into QQQ from risk-parity rebalancing. Watch DXY—if USD rolls over sharply, that could relieve pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (futures -2%, NAS100 -0.33% intraday weakness, sector rotation active). Recommend WAITING for retest of intraday support (~30,100–30,150) or a 4-hour close above 30,400 before re-entering longs. For shorts, consider partial profit-taking into 30,000 round level; move not yet extended enough to chase further downside aggressively. Next pivotal level: US PMI release later today.

factors.name: Global Tech Selloff
factors.summary: South Korea's Kospi Index plunged 10% from record highs; Nasdaq futures down 2.29% to 29,950 per FXStreet 08:36. Bloomberg reports 'Tech Losses Pile Up as Investors Focus on AI Risks' with cascading selloff across global tech stocks.

factors.name: AI Capex Cost Concerns
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs warns 'investor assumptions about the AI trade are starting to stretch reality' (11:29); Engram AI startup raising capital on token cost concerns suggests margin pressure. SpaceX dropped ~20% partly attributed to $20B debt refinance for xAI acquisition, signaling high capex concerns.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Fed dot plot last week projected 2026 rate hikes; market increased hike bets to 38 bps per investinglive. Hawkish Fed expectations eroding long-duration growth multiple appeal and supporting USD strength (DXY near fresh highs).

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: US Dollar at fresh highs since May 2025; real yields (implied by 10Y Treasury and Fed tightening bias) rising. Long-duration Nasdaq components highly sensitive to real rate compression — this is bearish for growth multiples.

factors.name: Earnings Guidance & Layoffs
factors.summary: Oracle shed 21,000 roles citing AI deployment; Google's online dominance 'showing signs of cracking in AI era' (12:00). Sentiment shift from capex enthusiasm to profitability concerns and workforce reductions.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Individual Risk
factors.summary: SpaceX (fresh IPO, not Nasdaq but sentiment indicator) down >4% and lost $400B in value; Nike stock slipping. Tech concentration risk and single-name deterioration spreading across growth names.

factors.name: Goldman Sachs Valuation Concern
factors.summary: Goldman strategist Rich Privorotsky notes 'rubber band' market stretched on AI; simultaneous warnings from multiple strategists suggest narrative shift from euphoria to risk reassessment.

factors.name: Bond Market Stress Signals
factors.summary: Apollo private credit fund capping redemptions after exit requests hit 17%; credit market liquidity concerns emerging. This often precedes equity volatility.

factors.name: Prior Haiku Bias Alignment
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL reads (5/10 and 6/10) on 2026-06-23 suggest technical weakness already identified before major selloff acceleration in premarket. Current downside continuation aligns with prior technical setup.

factors.name: Volatility Rising
factors.summary: VXN (Nasdaq vol) implied elevated; crypto correlation showing stress (Bitcoin breaks below 63,750 support, hitting 11-day lows). Risk-off mode spreading.

key_levels.support: 29,950 (premarket futures low per FXStreet 08:36); 29,500 (psychological); 29,000 (weekly trend support)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in the early-to-middle phase of a tech-led selloff driven by three converging pressures: (1) AI capex cost-inflation concerns after goldilocks narrative breaks down; (2) Fed hawkish repricing raising real yields and compressing growth multiples; (3) global contagion from South Korea's 10% Kospi plunge and Oracle/Google earnings disappointments. Premarket futures down 2.3% suggest opening gap-down is likely, with institutional repositioning away from mega-cap growth. Prior three Haiku SELL reads (5-6/10, unconfirmed) provided early warning; confirmation now arriving through volume and breadth deterioration.
catalysts_ahead: US S&P Global PMI flash (Tuesday 10:00 EDT) — economic health gauge; miss = more Fed cut bets, hit = supports hawkish stance
FOMC Minutes (if released this week) — Fed communication on rate path and AI/inflation narrative
Upcoming mega-cap earnings (TSLA, NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, META Q2) — guidance on capex/AI spend critical; misses will extend selloff
10-year Treasury yield (currently implied elevated) — every 10bp move impacts NDX multiples ~1-2%
China manufacturing data / geopolitical escalation (Taiwan, Iran nuclear talks uncertain) — risk-off tail risk
screening.risk_warning: If US PMI data comes in stronger than expected OR Fed officials walk back hawkish tone in coming 24h, real yields could compress and growth multiples could stabilize. S&P 500 stabilization (if support holds around 7,430) could limit Nasdaq downside. Conversely, if premarket opens gap-down and breaks 29,950 on high volume, cascade to 29,000-29,500 likely; watch for capitulation reversal signal (extreme VXN spike, put/call ratio extremes).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (early-to-mid selloff, 2-3h into cascade); confidence 7 but not max conviction due to premarket stage. Best entry on retest of 30,200-30,300 resistance after initial gap-down open, or wait for stabilization signal near 29,500-29,750 support before adding shorts. Do NOT chase market-open panic — liquidity poorest first 30min; wait 30-60min for volume profile to establish.

factors.name: Global Tech Selloff
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports S&P 500 futures down 1.3%, Nasdaq futures down over 2% amid global tech rout. South Korea's Kospi fell 10% from record high on chip stock selloff (Samsung, SK Hynix). This is contagion spreading globally across tech heavyweights.

factors.name: AI Cost & Monetization Concerns
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs warns investor assumptions about AI trade 'stretching reality'. Google's online dominance showing cracks in AI era per CNBC. Engram raises $98M focused on cutting AI token costs, highlighting rising expense problem for AI models.

factors.name: SpaceX Post-IPO Collapse
factors.summary: SpaceX shed $400 billion in value, stock below IPO-day close. Anyone who bought after first trading day underwater. This represents extreme repricing of mega-cap tech valuations and signals broader re-rating of stretched multiples.

factors.name: Oracle Mass Layoffs
factors.summary: Oracle shed 21,000 roles over past year amid AI-driven workforce reductions. Company stated 'AI deployment may continue to result in workforce reductions' - this signals margin pressure and operational stress at major tech players.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: USD strength continues after hawkish Fed dot plot last week projecting rate hike this year (consensus was no change). Market pricing 38bps of tightening. Kevin Warsh steering Fed toward less forward guidance, stronger price stability emphasis per BNY.

factors.name: Rising Real Yields
factors.summary: 10-year yields rising on hawkish Fed repricing. Long-duration growth stocks (NDX core) extremely sensitive to real rate increases. Higher discount rates compress valuations on high-multiple tech names.

factors.name: Apollo Private Credit Liquidity Crisis
factors.summary: Apollo capping redemptions in main retail private credit fund after exit requests hit 17%. Reignites broader liquidity fears and risk-off sentiment that typically hits high-beta tech hardest.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Asia chip stocks nose-diving (Korea down 10%). Bitcoin correlation turning bearish alongside chip stocks per investing.com. Semiconductor weakness is direct negative for NDX given NVDA, AMD, INTC, AVGO weighting.

factors.name: Crypto Market Spillover
factors.summary: Bitcoin drops below $62k to 11-day low, crypto market selling off as Nasdaq tech selloff spills into digital assets per CoinDesk. Risk-off sentiment across correlated high-beta assets reinforces tech weakness.

factors.name: Valuation Concerns
factors.summary: Investing.com notes SpaceX 'looked stretched relative to other mega-cap companies even at IPO level'. Broad re-rating underway. Researchers reportedly cracked code on predicting bubbles per MarketWatch, raising bubble concerns.

factors.name: AI Buildout Cost Uncertainty
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures specifically cited as dropping 'over 2% on concerns over AI buildout costs, Fed rate outlook' per Stock Market News. Core driver is uncertainty over capex returns and margin pressure from AI investments.

confirmation.narrative: A global tech rout is underway with Nasdaq futures down 2%+ as Asian markets led a selloff (Korea -10% on chip stocks). Multiple bearish catalysts converging: SpaceX's $400B wipeout signals valuation re-rating, Goldman warns AI assumptions 'stretch reality', Oracle's 21k layoffs show AI margin pressure, and hawkish Fed (38bps hike priced) lifts real yields crushing long-duration tech multiples. Apollo's liquidity crisis and crypto weakness reinforce broad risk-off. This is Phase 2 of a legitimate de-risking event, not mere surface noise.
catalysts_ahead: US PMI data release today (11:00 AM window)
Ongoing US-Iran nuclear uncertainty (Iran denied IAEA inspection claims)
Tech earnings season approaching
Fed speakers this week on rate path
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if US PMI data surprises significantly to upside showing resilient economy OR if major de-escalation breakthrough in Middle East triggers risk-on reversal OR if Fed speakers walk back hike expectations. Oversold bounce possible given -2% futures move already realized.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (futures -2%, Phase 2). Best risk/reward is waiting for bounce to 30,450-30,500 resistance zone before adding short exposure, or confirmation below 30,200 on cash open with tight stops above 30,350.

screening.current_price: ~19,200 (inferred from -320pt NASDAQ losses mentioned in premarket/intraday)

factors.name: Tech sector broad selloff
factors.summary: Micron down 8.86%, Nvidia down 2.73%, SpaceX down 4 consecutive days (briefly below $150 IPO price). Semiconductor weakness (KLA, Credo tumbling) signals capex/demand hesitation.

factors.name: Fed hawkish pivot confirmed
factors.summary: Markets repricing for October rate hike after Warsh Fed meeting; DXY at one-year highs, USD/EUR broke key support (EUR below 1.1400 for first time since June 2025). Higher-for-longer narrative crushes long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real yields rising (USD strength proxy)
factors.summary: Gold down (near $4,100), silver down >4%, precious metals pressured by rising rate expectations. Real rates likely moving higher, inversely correlated with Nasdaq growth multiples.

factors.name: AI capex & supply chain concerns
factors.summary: Micron and semiconductor downturn may signal AI capex slowdown or inventory correction. No offsetting positive guidance from hyperscalers in news window.

factors.name: Macro weakness & employment erosion
factors.summary: Richmond Fed composite +4 vs +13 prior; employment down (-1 vs +3 prior); factory job cuts in June neared 2008/COVID levels per S&P. Oracle shedding 21,000 roles, Ethereum Foundation cutting 40% budget & 20% staff.

factors.name: Risk-off sentiment in equities
factors.summary: Broader indices gapped lower, partial recovery still down 300+pts (NDX) from open. SpaceX/crypto volatility (Bitcoin below $62K) reinforces risk-off mood in tech/growth.

factors.name: PMI mixed but not supportive
factors.summary: US S&P Global Composite PMI 52.2 (beat 51.5 prior), Manufacturing PMI 55.7 (beat 54.8), but employment fell second month running. Growth modest, cost pressures up. Not a growth catalyst.

factors.name: Oil decline & geopolitical relief
factors.summary: Iran sanctions waiver, Strait of Hormuz reopening pressuring crude (below $80, touched $76.5). Supports lower inflation expectations but not enough to offset Fed hawkish repricing.

factors.name: Bias history continuity
factors.summary: Prior reads 2026-06-23 04:59 (SELL 5/10), 08:33 (SELL 5/10), 12:06 (SELL 7/10 → Sonnet SELL 8/10). Current newsflow reinforces downside bias with added conviction.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq has rolled over hard into a multi-cycle sell as the Fed's hawkish repricing (October hike odds rising, real yields up, DXY at 1-year highs) crushes long-duration tech multiples. Semiconductor sector crumbling (Micron -8.86%, KLA, Credo down) signals potential AI capex slowdown or inventory correction, while mega-cap earnings looming without forward guidance support. Employment, manufacturing shipments, and service sentiment all declining YoY despite PMI beats, painting a picture of macro deceleration into tighter monetary conditions. The move is already underway (down 300+ pts intraday), but breadth weakness and risk-off spillover (crypto, SPX, VXN rising) suggest further downside to test key support.
catalysts_ahead: Micron forward guidance / earnings call (imminent, likely market-moving for semis and AI narrative)
FOMC-adjacent commentary from Warsh and other Fed speakers (supply-side focus, real rates emphasis)
AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings in coming weeks (mega-cap guidance on capex, cloud, AI demand)
Core PCE, CPI prints (inflation trajectory; if hot → more hawkish repricing; if cool → modest relief)
VIX/VXN elevation & options gamma (watch for dealer hedging unwinding or options-driven rallies)
10-year Treasury yield: if stays >4.4%, real rates remain elevated and structural headwind for NDX
screening.risk_warning: If Micron or other semis provide upbeat AI capex commentary and guidance, or if Fed speakers suddenly pivot dovish on real rate concerns, the SELL thesis could reverse quickly. Additionally, if a major tech earnings beat (MSFT, GOOGL cloud growth) contradicts macro weakness narrative, short-covering and momentum could drive sharp snap-back rally. Watch VXN for extremes (>30 = potential capitulation/flush).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already -300+pts intraday; await retest of 19,250–19,350 (intraday resistance) before adding shorts. Alternatively, shorts into any modest bounce are favored; break below 19,050 opens acceleration to 18,900. For longs: wait for 2-3% bounce + confirmation (VXN drop, tech breadth reversal) before re-entering.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway with partial rebound from -800pts premarket low; wait for bounce to 20,000-20,100 resistance zone before adding shorts or enter on break below today's session lows.

factors.name: Fed hawkish tilt / rate hike expectations
factors.summary: Market pricing 60% chance of Fed hike by September, 28bps by year-end. New Fed Chair Warsh described as 'supply side economist' shifting policy framework. October hike bets reinforced after last week's meeting—compresses tech multiples significantly.

factors.name: Treasury yields / Real yields rising
factors.summary: USD at one-year highs driving real yields higher, creating severe pressure on long-duration growth stocks. Tech sector is extremely sensitive to real rate moves; this is a primary compression driver for NQ multiples.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector collapse
factors.summary: Micron down 8.86%, NVDA down 2.73%, Credo and KLA both tumbling. MarketWatch reports 'gut-check moment for AI stocks' with tech selloff led by memory/chip names. This is core NQ exposure bleeding badly.

factors.name: SpaceX / High-profile tech rout
factors.summary: SpaceX stock fell below $150 IPO debut price (briefly), down 4th consecutive day amid '$600 billion wipeout.' Bloomberg reports tech selloff with SpaceX as poster child for post-IPO euphoria reversal. Risk-off in high-multiple names.

factors.name: Google's AI dominance cracking
factors.summary: CNBC reports 'Google's online dominance showing signs of cracking in AI era,' complicating the GOOGL story despite position of strength per Wall Street. Structural risk to mega-cap tech thesis.

factors.name: Oracle mass layoffs (AI-driven)
factors.summary: Oracle shed 21,000 roles over past year amid wave of AI layoffs. Company explicitly cited 'adoption and deployment of AI technologies' causing workforce reductions—signals margin pressure and efficiency demands across tech.

factors.name: US PMI data (mixed, slight beat)
factors.summary: June flash Manufacturing PMI 55.7 vs 54.8 expected (49-month high), Services 51.3 vs 51.0, Composite 52.2 vs 51.7 prior. However, employment fell for second month and growth remains sluggish. Doesn't materially change Fed calculus or tech outlook.

factors.name: USD strength (DXY at 1-year highs)
factors.summary: Dollar at one-year highs pressures NQ mega-caps with heavy international revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA all >40% revenue ex-US). EUR/USD below 1.14, CAD at multi-month lows—broad USD rally is structural headwind.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (BTC breakdown)
factors.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $60K signaling 'Fed, ETF and AI pressures' per Deutsche Bank. BTC-NQ correlation on risk-off days remains intact; crypto weakness amplifies tech sector risk-off sentiment.

factors.name: ETF flows / Market structure risk
factors.summary: Report notes 'NASDAQ futures at one point down more than 800 points in premarket'—extreme gap lower suggests institutional de-risking and potential QQQ/XLK outflows. Partial recovery indicates some dip-buying but sellers still in control per commentary.

factors.name: Apollo redemption pressure / Private credit stress
factors.summary: Apollo capping withdrawals from flagship fund after redemptions far above 5% limit. Broader private credit stress signals liquidity concerns and risk-off institutional behavior—indirect but material tech sector headwind.

factors.name: Positive nuclear buildout / AI infra
factors.summary: Trump admin loaning $17B to speed deployment of 10 big nuclear reactors; Oklo targeting power production in ~2 years. Supports AI data center thesis, but this is long-term structural vs. immediate sentiment/flows.

factors.name: Iran sanctions waiver / Oil down
factors.summary: US issued sweeping rollback of Iran oil sanctions, Brent briefly touched $76.5. Lower energy costs marginally positive for consumer discretionary, but minimal NQ impact given sector composition. Macro stabilization theme partially offset by Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Ethereum Foundation cuts / Crypto sector stress
factors.summary: Ethereum Foundation cutting 20% of workforce (54 jobs) amid leadership exodus and 40% budget cut announced by Vitalik. Crypto sector stress bleeds into tech sentiment given overlap in investor base and risk appetite.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 is under severe pressure from a confluence of hawkish Fed repricing (60% chance September hike), surging real yields/USD at 1-year highs compressing tech multiples, and a semiconductor sector collapse led by Micron (-8.86%) and NVDA (-2.73%). SpaceX's high-profile breakdown below IPO price symbolizes broader post-euphoria correction in high-multiple tech names. While premarket panic (-800pts) has partially recovered (-320pts at present), technical damage is done with gap lower and sellers in control per market commentary. The 'gut-check moment for AI stocks' theme dominates, and despite some long-term positives (nuclear buildout, lower oil), near-term sentiment and flows favor further downside.
catalysts_ahead: Micron earnings today/this week (already showing volatility per news)
Any Fed speaker commentary reinforcing or walking back hike expectations
Thursday/Friday: potential month-end rebalancing flows
Next week: PCE data (key Fed inflation gauge)
Ongoing SpaceX price action as sentiment bellwether for high-multiple tech
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed speakers push back on September hike pricing, driving USD/yields lower; (2) Micron earnings surprise positively and reverse semiconductor rout; (3) NAS100 reclaims 20,100 and holds above pre-gap levels on strong volume, indicating institutional dip-buying overwhelms sellers. Extended move from overnight lows means immediate chase risk is elevated—better entries likely on any relief bounce.

factors.name: Tech sector selloff underway
factors.summary: Semiconductor stocks plunging (Micron -8.86%, Nvidia -2.73% per sector overview); chip stocks broadly under pressure. Move is already in progress with broad tech weakness persisting.

factors.name: Fed rate hike expectations rising
factors.summary: USD jumped to highest since November on Fed hike bets; Dollar strength typically pressures high-duration growth stocks. Treasury 2-year auction at 4.189% suggests elevated rate expectations ahead.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration risk
factors.summary: Higher nominal rates + stronger dollar environment compress valuations on NDX-heavy mega-cap growth names (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, TSLA). Real yields rising on Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Meta / AI regulation headwinds
factors.summary: Meta building prediction markets app 'Arena' sparked selling in regulatory-sensitive tech names; adds to AI compliance uncertainty and sector-specific sentiment deterioration.

factors.name: SpaceX correction / risk-off sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX fell to near IPO price after 3-day losing streak with ~$600B valuation wipeout noted; signals broader risk-off in high-beta growth, correlates to broader NQ weakness and crypto (BTC down to $62K).

factors.name: Cryptocurrency weakness (BTC $62K support test)
factors.summary: Bitcoin teasing breakdown below $62K amid Fed hawkishness and AI stock volatility; NQ-BTC correlation strong on risk-off days, BTC weakness signals risk sentiment deterioration.

factors.name: Ethereum Foundation cuts 20% staff
factors.summary: ETH Foundation workforce reduction amid crypto uncertainty; secondary signal of sector consolidation and reduced conviction in near-term crypto/Web3 growth narratives tied to tech.

factors.name: Prior analyst bias (SELL 7-8/10 sustained)
factors.summary: Your reads from 12:06 and 15:47 on 2026-06-23 consistently flagged SELL 7-8/10; this new pass reconfirms prior bias, adding conviction to sustained bearish setup.

factors.name: Limited near-term bullish catalysts in news
factors.summary: SpaceX +6% intraday bounce and nuclear/infrastructure spending announcements present, but overshadowed by tech selloff and rate concerns. No major mega-cap earnings catalyst until later this week.

factors.name: Micron earnings volatility ahead
factors.summary: Micron forward earnings expected soon; high volatility risk could trigger sharp intraday swings. Not yet released, so directional bias unclear but exacerbates near-term whipsaws.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in PHASE 2 of a tech-driven selloff, down -0.63% in 24h and flat 4h amid persistent semiconductor weakness (Micron -8.86%, chip sector broadly red) and rising Fed rate-hike expectations (USD at 1-yr highs, 2-yr auction strong). Real yield compression fears on growth multiples, combined with Meta regulatory headlines, SpaceX correction ($600B wipeout), and crypto weakness (BTC testing $62K), have kept risk sentiment firmly risk-off. Your prior SELL 7-8/10 reads sustain; move is already underway with limited bounce conviction so far.
catalysts_ahead: Micron forward earnings (imminent, per news)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (likely later this week, dates TBD)
FOMC minutes / Fed speaker commentary (Kevin Warsh Atlanta Fed selection process underway)
CPI print (next scheduled, usually 2nd week of month — watch for inflation surprises that re-price Fed hike odds)
PCE / Core PCE data (late June / early July)
NFP / jobless claims (next week, watch for labor market cooling that could ease Fed urgency)
screening.risk_warning: If Micron earnings beat and guide higher on AI capex demand, or if a surprise dovish Fed comment emerges (Kevin Warsh signals pause), or if mega-cap earnings surprise to upside with strong forward guidance on cloud/AI capex, bias could flip to NEUTRAL/BUY. Also monitor Treasury yields; if 10-yr drops sharply on safe-haven bid or growth fears, that could ease multiple compression. Currently, move is in-progress so chasing shorts here is phase-2 risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway; wait for NAS100 to retrace to 29500–29700 resistance before considering fresh shorts. If support breaks decisively below 29200, next target is 29000. Do NOT chase lower here; volatility into Micron earnings is high, so size accordingly and be ready for whipsaws.
